Over a year ago, I wrote an urgent email to patriots just like you about what's really happening to January 6 Peaceful Protesters in the Washington, DC jail.

**Patriot, what 's being done to them is so demonic, so foul, that they're BEGGING to be transferred to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba...where vicious, America-hating terrorists get better treatment!**

I'm heartsick and deeply angry about what's being done to our fellow patriots.

Their letter reads like an appeal from dissidents in the Soviet Union.

Here are just a few examples of what they're enduring (I quote directly from their letter, published by Gateway Pundit):

* **Begging for Help / Water / Medical Aid / Mercy****through a 4 inch by 10 inch window of cold metal doors**
* No visits from family, friends, lawyers, or clergy
* No Mass or Sunday worship service
* Laundry returning with brown stains, pubic hair, and or reeking of urine
* Broken toilets that either wont flush or repeatedly explode in Cells
* Stuck in Cells for 9 days without shower
* Solitary Confinement for 25 1⁄2 hours or more at a time

But there's more.

**Guards deny them hot water, and keep them from going to court. They severely beat, mace, harass, insult, and even sexually assault them.**

Their food is so disgusting and so low in nutrition, that some prisoners are losing their eyesight, their hair, and getting scurvy (from Vitamin C deficiency).

There's much, much more...all of it horrifying.

And this has been going on for years ... in AMERICA.

**They conclude their letter by pleading to be transferred to Guantanamo Bay, Cuba, where the Taliban and Islamic terrorists get better treatment!**

This can't go on.

**That 's why I've filed crucial lawsuits to FREE January 6 peaceful protesters. Including a critical new lawsuit that PROVES the FBI and other agencies carried out ILLEGAL SURVEILLANCE on January 6 patriots. That means their criminal convictions can be THROWN OUT. And their criminal trials MUST STOP.**

I'm Larry Klayman, founder and general counsel for Freedom Watch.

I'm a former federal prosecutor and trial lawyer who's fought for the American people for 41 years.

You've seen me on CNN, Fox News, and other cable news outlets.

body:
This initiative is not merely about economic reform.

This sponsored message from Conservatives Give is on behalf of Hillsdale College

| | [](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=2b5497a27f4349818fc104a9fbff5521&_e=Bg0Q1I-fC7X5SVv2YltCew3pA_D1QlT6AbOhXSfzzRkZ48A9XCBoAnQ4pO8q9wd26FOVXEO5encHIn6Xcri0vAAvTxdgdiGDMEI23CHYGpYuUAyQtxXBOdymKvzToFiJo6tPS_ivTtXpD_th3Vc8nJcLklpFhTRgOSb2eCDnacQ3NtKgiRAvhlSwEKA7fpSMAKlvHBCTPcpiVpD3CHe1j3G5-U7IUF9WNyZW7D67GxoUbBxMqi7UGeqOiHCFhXNOVxa4P2By3xp5tHjZ_t38rPwOALOm6Ty_1BgrqQPjtcwxDoci3FrLUVZCJLMDO5TAe5Uw_wpnPADauoRpAAELXK7vQ8tV5kkplG2d-rL5YlrfafE_f7W_zj06SIkrIPZpBM5kX_hDdbVDb_orYJY8YQ%3D%3D) Dear Fellow American,

A few years ago, members of the world's elite met at the annual World Economic Forum in Davos, Switzerland, and launched the Great Reset initiative.

What is their plan, and what does it mean for America?

In short, they aim to transform the world economy. As Klaus Schwab--the founder and executive director of the Forum--wrote, "We need a 'Great Reset' of capitalism." If successful, their plan would also have the effect of destroying our American way of life.

This initiative is not merely about economic reform. It is an attempt to impose global rule by so-called experts.

Some social media companies are censoring our efforts to promote a seminar we hosted on the Hillsdale campus that explored the topic of the Great Reset. Fortunately, we are able to communicate directly with you.

In this seminar, we consider the idea of the Great Reset and its implications for America. But that's not all. We also delve into pro-freedom alternatives to this tyrannical plan.

**Sign up now using the secure link below, and we 'll send the Great Reset seminar lectures--absolutely free--directly to your inbox.**

[https://lp.hillsdale.edu/the-great-reset-cca/](https://links.conservativesgive.com/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=2b5497a27f4349818fc104a9fbff5521&_e=Bg0Q1I-fC7X5SVv2YltCew3pA_D1QlT6AbOhXSfzzRkZ48A9XCBoAnQ4pO8q9wd26FOVXEO5encHIn6Xcri0vAAvTxdgdiGDMEI23CHYGpYuUAyQtxXBOdymKvzToFiJo6tPS_ivTtXpD_th3Vc8nJcLklpFhTRgOSb2eCDnacQ3NtKgiRAvhlSwEKA7fpSMAKlvHBCTPcpiVpD3CHe1j3G5-U7IUF9WNyZW7D67GxoUbBxMqi7UGeqOiHCFhXNODdoz4v8AgKBjyZfxvunsxlq4i-VWV6T0CDE0ZPdqOvLN9mnHqkZ65mQygk5pQzOIpUhlLOG0CXJdY7TTCuWYP_DPANEWw9r0GHDY8SdJApK5YYRvYaCjQroMY4iUZCVEMWLOceUr3XXhBa-BJwMVSw%3D%3D)

Don't miss this opportunity to learn lessons that are essential to the defense of American liberty.

Use the link above to receive your Great Reset video series today.

Warm regards,

Larry P. Arnn
President, Hillsdale College
Pursuing Truth and Defending Liberty Since 1844
---

| | Friend, It’s official. We are through game one of the double header. The voters have spoken, and they chose me to be the Republican candidate for Senate in California. That means you and I will have just eight months to build a 58-county, grassroots campaign capable of defeating Adam Schiff - with his _ActBlue_ allies and the war chest he's assembled during the two decades he spent in Washington, D.C. **[That why I’m humbly asking you to rush and emergency $35 contribution to my campaign today.](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/Dkz8_b8kE3apBU1ubmPfpA~~/AARclAA~/RgRn2WZ2P0SsaHR0cHM6Ly9zZWN1cmUud2lucmVkLmNvbS9zdGV2ZS1nYXJ2ZXktZm9yLXVzLXNlbmF0ZS9wYTdjMzMyMmViOGJlNzY_dXRtX3NvdXJjZT1CREctUkVWJnV0bV9tZWRpdW09RU0mdXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPTIwMjQwMzEzX0JER19TR0NBX1lPR0kzX1JFVl9QUi1QQS1ST04mdXRtX3RjPVJPTi0yMDI0MDMxN1cDc3BjQgpl7zjK9mVDd-iPUhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)** There’s no time to lose. With every day that goes by, you and I are getting more and more reminders why it’s so important we win this election. The reality is present-day America is starting to look a lot like 1970s America. You remember what it was like: ► Not only were gas prices sky high, Carter's rationing meant we could only buy gas on even or odd days depending on our license plate numbers. Remember? ► Eleven Israeli athletes were slaughtered at the Olympic Village in Munich by terrorists. Remember? ► Fifty-two Americans were held hostage for 444 days by terrorists in Iran. Remember? Democrat policies had brought America to its knees. And now, as Yogi Berra used to say, **_"It's deja vu all over again."_** Joe Biden, Adam Schiff and the rest of the Democrats don't care one bit that Americans are having a hard time. I'd go even further to say they welcome the misery so they can keep folks down and dependent. clean_body: Help me hit a home run | | | ~~ ~~ | | Friend, It’s official. We are through game one of the double header. The voters have spoken, and they chose me to be the Republican candidate for Senate in California. That means you and I will have just eight months to build a 58-county, grassroots campaign capable of defeating Adam Schiff - with his ActBlue allies and the war chest he's assembled during the two decades he spent in Washington, D.C. ~~ There’s no time to lose. With every day that goes by, you and I are getting more and more reminders why it’s so important we win this election. The reality is present-day America is starting to look a lot like 1970s America. You remember what it was like: ► Not only were gas prices sky high, Carter's rationing meant we could only buy gas on even or odd days depending on our license plate numbers. Remember? ► Eleven Israeli athletes were slaughtered at the Olympic Village in Munich by terrorists. Remember? ► Fifty-two Americans were held hostage for 444 days by terrorists in Iran. Remember? Democrat policies had brought America to its knees. And now, as Yogi Berra used to say, "It's deja vu all over again." Joe Biden, Adam Schiff and the rest of the Democrats don't care one bit that Americans are having a hard time. I'd go even further to say they welcome the misery so they can keep folks down and dependent. You and I must win this race. ~~ | | Sincerely, | | ~~ Steve Garvey #6 | ~~ | | ~~ | | PAID FOR BY STEVE GARVEY FOR U.S. SENATE PO Box 532389Los Angeles, CA 90053-2389 | ** Restore Our Nation PO Box 1177 Fort Collins, CO 80522 | ~~_

body:
Remember when the FBI declared parents angry at school boards for inappropriate books were terrorists?

Friend,

Remember when the FBI declared parents angry at school boards for inappropriate books were terrorists? Parents were rightfully angry at what they saw in schools.

But have you ever wondered, "Who is putting these inappropriate books in schools for kids?"

My buddy Kirk Cameron did some research and discovered a shocking truth about the culprit putting disgusting materials in school libraries.

It's not Woke teachers (although they don't help).
It's not even school boards.
It's Scholastic Book Fairs.

Now, you are probably like me. You may have fond memories of book fairs by Scholastic in your youth. You may have gotten a hold of books like Where the Red Fern Grows, Huckleberry Finn, and other classics.

Scholastic is now promoting books like _Welcome to St. Hell_ , which promotes trans ideology to kids.

[](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/5cY2_UTIsQn9M7lXJZG4-Q~~/AARclAA~/RgRn2W0kP0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9za3l0cmVlLWJvb2stZmFpcnMucmV2di5jby9wYS1kb25hdGU_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWpvaG5fcmljaCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPXJldnNoYXJlJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9cGEmdXRtX3RjPUNCMi0yMDI0MDMxNyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1zdF9oZWxsX2ltYWdlMVcDc3BjQgpl7yPR9mVEqhA6UhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)[](http://post.spmailtechnol.com/f/a/VwdXGWqwZMOcpPoNAXuiiA~~/AARclAA~/RgRn2W0kP0SUaHR0cHM6Ly9za3l0cmVlLWJvb2stZmFpcnMucmV2di5jby9wYS1kb25hdGU_dXRtX2NhbXBhaWduPWpvaG5fcmljaCZ1dG1fbWVkaXVtPXJldnNoYXJlJnV0bV9zb3VyY2U9cGEmdXRtX3RjPUNCMi0yMDI0MDMxNyZ1dG1fY29udGVudD1zdF9oZWxsX2ltYWdlMlcDc3BjQgpl7yPR9mVEqhA6UhRkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbVgEAAAAAA~~)

I joined Kirk Cameron recently in Nashville to promote wholesome and age-appropriate book fairs for kids. It's called SkyTree Book Fairs, and Kirk helped jumpstart this amazing organization.

SkyTree Book Fairs offers schools a different option for book fairs. SkyTree Book Fairs is providing schools, parents, and children with an empowering alternative with literature rooted in truth and family values that matches the quality and ease of Scholastic while countering their harmful agenda.

Here's how you can help.

[Please help us promote new book fairs in schools to replace Scholastic. Can you donate $35, $50, or even $100 today toward SkyTree? I am hoping to raise $50,000. The Woke and radically leftwing Scholastic holds a virtual monopoly on school book fairs. Like many of our cultural institutions in the last decade, Scholastic has fallen prey to the woke mob and radical LGBT political agenda.

SkyTree will bring back wholesome content to kids, like what we had growing up. Kids should remain innocent and have content to encourage their moral character while fostering their imagination.

I once said if a teacher is doing their job correctly, you should never know about their politics, details of their personal life, or cultural stances.

It's the same with our kids' literature.

clean_body: Remember when the FBI declared parents angry at school boards for inappropriate books were terrorists? Friend, Remember when the FBI declared parents angry at school boards for inappropriate books were terrorists? Parents were rightfully angry at what they saw in schools. But have you ever wondered, "Who is putting these inappropriate books in schools for kids?" My buddy Kirk Cameron did some research and discovered a shocking truth about the culprit putting disgusting materials in school libraries. It's not Woke teachers (although they don't help). It's not even school boards. It's Scholastic Book Fairs. Now, you are probably like me. You may have fond memories of book fairs by Scholastic in your youth. You may have gotten a hold of books like Where the Red Fern Grows, Huckleberry Finn, and other classics. Scholastic is now promoting books like Welcome to St. Hell , which promotes trans ideology to kids. ~~ I joined Kirk Cameron recently in Nashville to promote wholesome and age-appropriate book fairs for kids. It's called SkyTree Book Fairs, and Kirk helped jumpstart this amazing organization. SkyTree Book Fairs offers schools a different option for book fairs. SkyTree Book Fairs is providing schools, parents, and children with an empowering alternative with literature rooted in truth and family values that matches the quality and ease of Scholastic while countering their harmful agenda. Here's how you can help. ~ ~ ~ ~~ ~~ SkyTree Book Fairs are already operating in schools to provide wholesome content for families. They are spreading across the entire country, and I wanted to share some photos of their recent events with you. The Woke and radically leftwing Scholastic holds a virtual monopoly on school book fairs. Like many of our cultural institutions in the last decade, Scholastic has fallen prey to the woke mob and radical LGBT political agenda. SkyTree will bring back wholesome content to kids, like what we had growing up. Kids should remain innocent and have content to encourage their moral character while fostering their imagination. I once said if a teacher is doing their job correctly, you should never know about their politics, details of their personal life, or cultural stances. It's the same with our kids' literature. ~ ~ ~ ~~ ~~ Thank you, and God bless, John Rich P.S. Here's a photo of Kirk Cameron and I. Together, we are supporting SkyTree Book Fairs to promote wholesome, family-friendly books to kids in schools. SkyTree Book Fairs is a 501(c)(3) not-for-profit organization. Copyright SkyTree Book Fairs, 2023. Conservative Broadcast PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522 ~~ | ~~

name: The Office of Delegate Briana D. Sewell
subject: Del. Sewell's End of Session Newsletter
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell
body:
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/New%20Sewell%20Delegate%20Logo%20.png]
Sine Die Newsletter
Dear neighbors,
And just like that, the 2024 General Assembly session has concluded! But our work continues, and we’ll be back in Richmond in April for the reconvene session to consider the Governor’s actions on all the legislation we passed this session.
In the meantime, between the General Assembly adjourning and the reconvene session in April, delegates and senators will return to their districts to hear from constituents, provide legislative updates, take part in community events, and engage in important work in their communities.
I’m looking forward to coming home to District 25 and speaking directly with you about what we accomplished during the 2024 legislative session. If you’d like to have me at a community event or wish to schedule a meeting, please do not hesitate to send an event invitation or a meeting request to DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov] .
Constituent Services We Offer During the Interim
As we transition into the time in between legislative sessions (in Richmond, we refer to this as the ‘interim’), you may wonder what we work on during the time we are not actively legislating in session. One of the major focus areas for our office outside of session is offering constituent services to residents in our district. Below is a (non-comprehensive) list of some of the services we offer. I encourage you to take advantage of these services if you need assistance.
Help with State Agencies - If you are encountering challenges with a state agency and you need assistance, my office can assist with such matters as establishing contact with the agency, getting status on your request, or obtaining information on an inquiry, to name a few. Please reach out to our office and we will be happy to help liaise with the agency on your behalf. Agencies that we most often engage with include the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC), the Virginia Department of Taxation, and the Department of Motor Vehicles, but we can advocate on your behalf with any agency you may encounter. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if we can be of assistance.
Constituent Meetings - If you have questions or concerns about state government issues, or you just want to get to know me better as your delegate, please reach out and I will be happy to schedule a meeting with you.
Attend and Participate in Community Events - Schedule permitting, I am always happy to attend and/or participate in community events. If you would like me to attend and or participate in your community event or meeting, please contact my office. Be sure to provide as much advance notice as possible, and I will always put forth every effort to participate.
Recognition of Community Members and Organizations - As your representative in the Virginia House of Delegates, it is my pleasure to recognize residents and community organizations of Prince William County who have done amazing work in service to the community. During a special session, (a legislative session that is called between the end of regular legislative session and the beginning of the next regular legislative session), we have the ability to put together commending resolutions and memorial resolutions from the General Assembly in honor of community organization anniversaries, individual accomplishments, or members of the community who have passed away. If you have an individual or organization in mind that you think might be deserving of recognition, please send my team an email including the details of who should be recognized and their achievement. If a special session is called, we would love to work with you to put together a resolution in their honor. If no special session is called, we would be more than happy to send an official letter from the House of Delegates thanking them for their contributions to the community.
Seven Day Bills, Vetoes, and Reconvene Session
After the General Assembly adjourned “sine die,” on Saturday, March 9th, all bills passed during the legislative session moved to the final stage before a bill becomes a law, the governor’s desk. At this stage, bills can be signed by the governor (and become law), or they can be vetoed or amended. If the governor does not act on a bill by the deadline, then the bill becomes law automatically.
The governor is bound by different deadlines to act on legislation depending on when bills are communicated to him. According to the Virginia Constitution, if a bill that has passed the full General Assembly is presented to the Governor with more than seven days left in the session, then the Governor is required to take action on that bill within seven days. Bills that meet these criteria are informally known as seven day bills. If a bill is passed in the last seven days of a regular session, the governor has 30 days after the end of session to act on the bill.
Seven Day Bills
This year, the governor had until Friday, March 8 at 11:59 p.m. to take action on seven day bills. There were a total of 84 bills that were sent to the Governor that fell under the seven day deadline. Two of the bills I introduced are a part of that group, including:
HB738 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HB738] : my bill that will allow members serving in the U.S. Space Force to receive the same benefits and privileges as other members serving in other branches of the U.S. Armed Forces. The governor provided friendly amendments to this bill.
HB739 [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+sum+HB739] : my bill that will exempt childcare and early education programs that specifically serve military families from duplicative licensure requirements. The governor signed this bill into law.
Of the seven day bills communicated to the governor during the last week of session, 12 were vetoed, 8 were amended (including my HB738), and 64 were signed into law (including my HB739).
What happens now?
The seven day bills which were amended or vetoed are sent back to the General Assembly to approve the amendments or override the vetoes. The General Assembly must secure a 2/3rd vote from both chambers to override a governor veto or amendment. The seven day bills which were amended or vetoed will join all other bills which are vetoed or amended by the governor for reconsideration during the April reconvene session. Until then, the legislature must wait and see what the governor will do with the over a thousand bills upon which he must act by the 30 day deadline. You can find the full list of bills that have passed the General Assembly and are awaiting consideration by the Governor here [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?241+lst+SGN] .
Compromise Budget Heads to the Governor’s Desk
The compromise budget developed by the House and Senate negotiators was agreed to by both chambers of the General Assembly and has been sent to the governor’s desk.
I am happy to inform you that the final conference budget has been approved by both chambers of the General Assembly. This budget includes investments in various sectors such as education, mental health, healthcare, transportation, and housing. Below, please find highlights from the final House and Senate compromise budget.
Education
3% salary increase each year in the biennium for our public school teachers
$2.5 billion in additional funding for K-12 public education
$527.8 million over the biennium to support early childhood education
$371.3 million over the biennium to implement several JLARC recommendations
$72.1 million to provide support for ELL students
$205.4 million over the biennium to increase access, affordability and degree production for public colleges and universities. This works to limit in-state undergraduate tuition increases and includes $40 million in support for HBCUs
Economy/Working Families
$79.5 million over the biennium to support increasing the state’s minimum wage to $15/hr. by January 2026
3% salary increase each year of the biennium for state-supported employees
$175 million for the Virginia Housing Trust Fund to support the creation or preservation of affordable housing
$40 million over the biennium for the Virginia Business Ready Sites Program
$243.1 million over the biennium to backfill revenue lost from the removal of the grocery sales tax
Health/Mental Health
$30.5 million over the biennium to combat the opioid epidemic in our Commonwealth, including...
$11 million for the Virginia Department of Health to purchase opioid reversal agents and test kits
$400,000 for the Virginia Department of Health to conduct a waster water demonstration project to test for fentanyl and norfentanyl
$100,000 to equip public schools with naloxone to combat the deadly opioid epidemic
$191.4 million to add additional DD waiver slots and a 3% rate increase each year of the biennium for DD services
$126 million over the biennium in additional mental health services
$6.2 million over the biennium to fully fund community health workers in local health departments
$40 million over the biennium to incentivize higher staffing for nursing homes
Public Safety
$75.1 million over the biennium for community violence intervention initiatives, including....
$18 million over the biennium for the Community Violence Intervention and Prevention Program
$10 million for the Operation Ceasefire Grant Program
$7 million over the biennium to add Roanoke to the Safer Communities program
$6 million in additional support over the biennium for youth community violence programs
$21.3 million over the biennium to backfill and maintain funding for the VOCA Victims Services Grant Program and maintains funding for the Victim Witness, Sexual Assault, and Domestic Violence Victim and Victim Services grant programs
Natural Resources/Infrastructure
The budget directs the Governor to reenter the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative
$149.5 million over the biennium to improve our transportation system and ease the burden of the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority (WMATA)
$100 million for the Community Flood Preparedness Fund
$400 million to meet Chesapeake Bay improvement goals
$25 million for the City of Norfolk for Coastal Risk Storm Management Projects
Sine Die
Every year at the end of session we say the General Assembly adjourned ‘ sine die [https://www.dictionary.com/e/word-of-the-day/sine-die-2021-05-13/] ,’ but do you know what the Latin phrase means or where it comes from? Sine die directly translates from Latin to “without a day,” and it means without a day planned to come back to session. It designates the end of the official session for part-time legislatures. Although the Latin makes it sound like the phrase originated from Roman times, it actually derives from the 17th century and used to refer to legislative and court proceedings in British and American legal parlance. Despite the phrase being most properly pronounced in Latin as “seen-ay dee-yay,” the most popular pronunciation in the legislature is “sigh-knee dye.”
Welcome to Richmond! Constituent Engagement and More
Even though the final week of session was jam-packed, I’m glad I was able to meet with several constituents, organizations, and advocates. Thank you so much for stopping by!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/image000000.jpg] I had the honor of meeting with Lillie Jessie, a former member of the Prince William County School Board. Lillie and her family were in Richmond to accept a commending resolution from the House of Delegates for her tireless service to the students, families, and educators of Prince William County. Thank you, Lillie, for your leadership and congratulations on this well-deserved honor!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/IMG_0699_2.jpg] I also had the opportunity to meet with a representative from the Virginia Trucking Association [https://vatrucking.wildapricot.org/] to talk about what we can do at the state level to support fleet operators, vendors, and the Commonwealth’s trucking industry.
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/IMG_0701_2.jpg] One of my favorite parts of the session is the Page debate that takes place each year. The Pages take to the floor, propose their own legislation, debate policies, find compromise, and pass bills. This year’s debate did not disappoint, and I was once again impressed by the brilliant, thoughtful, and engaged young leaders we had serving as Pages this year. You can watch the full Page debate here [https://virginiageneralassembly.gov/house/chamber/chamberstream.php] .
Bills
I introduced a wide variety of bills this session. I am proud of the work we accomplished to make life better for service members and veterans, to ensure every child thrives, and to take care of working families. Here are my bills which are headed to Governor Glenn Youngkin’s desk:
PAID FAMILY AND MEDICAL LEAVE: This bill will establish a statewide, paid family and medical leave program. This bill would ensure that all Virginians have access to 8 weeks of paid leave when they need it to to care for a sick family member, recover from childbirth, take care of a new child, or recover from an illness themselves. This bill passed the General Assembly, 50 - 47 in the House and 20 - 19 in the State Senate. This bill has been communicated to the governor and he must sign, veto, or amend by 11:59 p.m., April 8, 2024.
ELIMINATE TEACHER LICENSURE BARRIERS: This bill works to fix the leaky teacher pipeline and increase teacher diversity through the elimination of a duplicative, expensive, and out-of-date testing requirement for teachers. This bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 99 - 0 in the House and 39 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill has been communicated to the governor and he must sign, veto, or amend by 11:59 p.m., April 8, 2024.
NALOXONE IN EVERY SCHOOL: This bill requires schools to stock and train staff (as well as encourage students to be trained) on the administration of Naloxone, a life-saving medication, in case of an opioid overdose. This bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 98 - 0 in the House and 39 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill will be communicated to the governor.
INVESTIGATE VETERANS AFFAIRS CLAIM SHARKS: This bill convenes a workgroup to investigate predatory Veterans Affairs benefits claim sharks. The final version of this bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 96 - 0 in the House and 40 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill will be communicated to the governor.
ALL SERVICES, ALL BENEFITS: This bill ensures all members of the armed forces have access to the same earned benefits no matter their service branch. This bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 99 - 0 in the House and 39 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill was a seven day bill acted on by the governor before the end of session. The governor indicated he would sign the bill with friendly amendments (to expand the bill to a few more code sections). The amendments will be accepted during reconvene and the bill should be signed into law and go into effect July 1st, 2024.
CHILD CARE FOR MILITARY FAMILIES: This bill attempts to address the military child care provider crisis by exempting from duplicative Virginia licensure DOD-licensed family providers which are based on military installations or serve only military families. This bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 96 - 0 in the House and 39 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill was a seven day bill acted on by the governor before the end of session. The governor signed the bill into law and it will go into effect July 1st, 2024.
RESTORE POWER FASTER: House Bill 1526 allows the DMV to issue overweight permits for an electric utility's operation of vehicles to allow more efficient uses of utility crews and vehicles and ensure power is returned more quickly during outages. This bill passed the General Assembly unanimously, 100 - 0 in the House and 40 - 0 in the State Senate. This bill has been communicated to the governor and he must sign, veto, or amend by 11:59 p.m., April 8, 2024.
This Week’s Mailbag
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/Screenshot%202024-03-04%20at%204.32.27%20PM.png]
My team and I have greatly appreciated all of the messages we received over the course of this legislative session. Whether it be feedback on proposed policies or shared viewpoints on various bills your voice was critical and your engagement remains essential to ensure a thriving democracy.
During the final days of the legislative session, we received emails about SB2, the proposed Assault Weapons Ban, vaping regulations, and legislation related to electric utilities.
One bill that received a lot of constituent interest was SB454, known by many in the General Assembly as the SMR bill. This bill would allow Appalachian Power and Dominion Energy to charge ratepayers for some of the development costs incurred in exploring the development of small modular nuclear reactor technology. Small modular nuclear reactors are new technology which have not yet been mass produced, vetted, or proven. One SMR development project in Idaho called NuScale was canceled because costs to consumers proved higher than expected. We received many constituents reaching out to share their concerns about this bill, and I am glad to have stood with them to vote no on the final version of the bill. Although I think investments in new technologies for energy generation are essential to stem the tide of climate change, I agree with the many constituents who reached out and I did not think this bill was ready yet to be passed in its final form. If you are interested in learning more about this subject, I encourage you to read more about the bill here [https://lis.virginia.gov/cgi-bin/legp604.exe?ses=241&typ=bil&val=sb454] .
Thank you for all your messages this session! However, I encourage you to stay engaged year round. Never hesitate to contact me or my team to share your ideas or concerns. And as we head towards the reconvene session in April, I hope you’ll remain active and stay in touch!
Contact Us!
Even though the 2024 legislative session is now over, and I am no longer in Richmond Monday through Friday, I am still your delegate. Post-session, you will find me in the district, meeting with residents, speaking with area businesses, visiting community organizations, discussing the impact of legislation passed during the 2024 legislative session, and working with constituents on many different types of state issues.
If you have questions or concerns about issues in the community or what your state government can do better to support you and advocate for changes that you would like to see, please don’t hesitate to reach out to my office. My staff and I will be glad to hear from you whether you are reaching out about a bill, a constituent service concern, or just sharing something that has happened in the community that you feel is important for me to know about.
My email is delbsewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@hous.virginia.gov] and my office number is (703) 261-4787. If you get these newsletters, but you have never reached out before, I encourage you to do so over the following months. If you are not sure how to engage with our office, but you want to know more about what we do, please shoot me an email, and below are a few ideas of ways you can engage. You can let me know about your feelings on community issues, share an event you might want me to attend in the community, or reach out to ask for a legislative update. No issue or request is too small for an email. Being available and of service to my constituents is very important to me.
Thank you so much for following along over the last sixty days. We have made critical progress on a whole host of issues and I’m proud of what we’ve accomplished on behalf of everyday Virginians. But there is much more to do, and I promise to keep you updated as that work continues!
[https://prod.cdn.everyaction.com/images/van/NGP/NGP13/1/87444/images/headingcommunitybill%20%281%29.png]
Paid for by Friends of Briana Sewell.
Friends of Briana Sewell
PO Box 1849
Woodbridge, VA 22195
United States
If you believe you received this message in error or wish to no longer receive email from us, please unsubscribe: https://secure.ngpvan.com/p/BDPKPY_4S0izjMklQV7G1w2?unsubscribedata=lYBYncTnyIg1Ua39NdK6l4BTpA3ZE74Cd50wIqnye2hSfqkQd3BBS%2By7Hz4iJajMuj8%2BzRxtArWpfF2JMPZ%2BjH%2B5w%2BUQU8vjnK1IgUryw%2B32pFczkjsYDjUH%2BvXEEs6N3DwV%2BNdbAc1w3fnuCZDOzmXVM3Ll6VUVbopqG37loOgY2QfKPBP%2Fyf64ORSWmvjZFsMHvlnBiR48fjeg%2Bt67nH9hBm8KpjEd5QCM%2FuOOLUgshjuRUpbtRxGqgssDqnkLbqvpxNy%2FY6zBDK9eei8IbX6fsDap7%2FT7XSb0xAMlr7h0ZMHpSATQVqCSn%2BaMMx4B9NfIZ3Ok6Lc8tXJ3FJQ8wN0bMABNBgcu7f5yl9F5e9qCu4n4svl1ostN%2F4cbzq2Zwv64MPso98S8MJWmVcE1sFHjzTGzWS7nqWlcW%2FC9X%2FUuq4Th5Rc%2B4ZQHITnaQlfh .
clean_body: [] Sine Die Newsletter Dear neighbors, And just like that, the 2024 General Assembly session has concluded! But our work continues, and we’ll be back in Richmond in April for the reconvene session to consider the Governor’s actions on all the legislation we passed this session. In the meantime, between the General Assembly adjourning and the reconvene session in April, delegates and senators will return to their districts to hear from constituents, provide legislative updates, take part in community events, and engage in important work in their communities. I’m looking forward to coming home to District 25 and speaking directly with you about what we accomplished during the 2024 legislative session. If you’d like to have me at a community event or wish to schedule a meeting, please do not hesitate to send an event invitation or a meeting request to DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov [DelBSewell@house.virginia.gov] . Constituent Services We Offer During the Interim As we transition into the time in between legislative sessions (in Richmond, we refer to this as the ‘interim’), you may wonder what we work on during the time we are not actively legislating in session. One of the major focus areas for our office outside of session is offering constituent services to residents in our district. Below is a (non-comprehensive) list of some of the services we offer. I encourage you to take advantage of these services if you need assistance. Help with State Agencies - If you are encountering challenges with a state agency and you need assistance, my office can assist with such matters as establishing contact with the agency, getting status on your request, or obtaining information on an inquiry, to name a few. Please reach out to our office and we will be happy to help liaise with the agency on your behalf. Agencies that we most often engage with include the Virginia Employment Commission (VEC), the Virginia Department of Taxation, and the Department of Motor Vehicles, but we can advocate on your behalf with any agency you may encounter. Please don’t hesitate to reach out if we can be of assistance. Constituent Meetings - If you have questions or concerns about state government issues, or you just want to get to know me better as your delegate, please reach out and I will be happy to schedule a meeting with you. Attend and Participate in Community Events - Schedule permitting, I am always happy to attend and/or participate in community events. If you would like me to attend and or participate in your community event or meeting, please contact my office. Be sure to provide as much advance notice as possible, and I will always put forth every effort to participate. Recognition of Community Members and Organizations - As your representative in the Virginia House of Delegates, it is my pleasure to recognize residents and community organizations of Prince William County who have done amazing work in service to the community. During a special session, (a legislative session that is called between the end of regular legislative session and the beginning of the next regular legislative session), we have the ability to put together commending resolutions and memorial resolutions from the General Assembly in honor of community organization anniversaries, individual accomplishments, or members of the community who have passed away. If you have an individual or organization in mind that you think might be deserving of recognition, please send my team an email including the details of who should be recognized and their achievement. If a special session is called, we would love to work with you to put together a resolution in their honor. If no special session is called, we would be more than happy to send an official letter from the House of Delegates thanking them for their contributions to the community. Seven Day Bills, Vetoes, and Reconvene Session After the General Assembly adjourned “sine die,” on Saturday, March 9th, all bills passed during the legislative session moved to the final stage before a bill becomes a law, the governor’s desk. At this stage, bills can be signed by the governor (and become law), or they can be vetoed or amended. If the governor does not act on a bill by the deadline, then the bill becomes law automatically. The governor is bound by different deadlines to act on legislation depending on when bills are communicated to him. According to the Virginia Constitution, if a bill that has passed the full General Assembly is presented to the Governor with more than seven days left in the session, then the Governor is required to take action on that bill within seven days. Bills that meet these criteria are informally known as seven day bills. If a bill is passed in the last seven days of a regular session, the governor has 30 days after the end of session to act on the bill. Seven Day Bills This year, the governor had until Friday, March 8 at 11:59 p.m. to take action on seven day bills. There were a total of 84 bills that were sent to the Governor that fell under the seven day deadline. Two of the bills I introduced are a part of that group, including: HB738 [] : my bill that will allow members serving in the U.S. Space Force to receive the same benefits and privileges as other members serving in other branches of the U.S. Armed Forces. The governor provided friendly amendments to this bill. HB739 [] : my bill that will exempt childcare and early education programs that specifically serve military families from duplicative licensure requirements. The governor signed this bill into law. Of the seven day bills communicated to the governor during the last week of session, 12 were vetoed, 8 were amended (including my HB738), and 64 were signed into law (including my HB739). What happens now? The seven day bills which were amended or vetoed are sent back to the General Assembly to approve the amendments or override the vetoes. The General Assembly must secure a 2/3rd vote from both chambers to override a governor veto or amendment. The seven day bills which were amended or vetoed will join all other bills which are vetoed or amended by the governor for reconsideration during the April reconvene session. Until then, the legislature must wait and see what the governor will do with the over a thousand bills upon which he must act by the 30 day deadline. You can find the full list of bills that have passed the General Assembly and are awaiting consideration by the Governor here [] . Compromise Budget Heads to the Governor’s Desk The compromise budget developed by the House and Senate negotiators was agreed to by both chambers of the General Assembly and has been sent to the governor’s desk. I am happy to inform you that the final conference budget has been approved by both chambers of the General Assembly. This budget includes investments in various sectors such as education, mental health, healthcare, transportation, and housing. Below, please find highlights from the final House and Senate compromise budget. Education 3% salary increase each year in the biennium for our public school teachers $2.5 billion in additional funding for K-12 public education $527.8 million over the biennium to support early childhood education $371.3 million over the biennium to implement several JLARC recommendations $72.1 million to provide support for ELL students $205.4 million over the biennium to increase access, affordability and degree production for public colleges and universities. This works to limit in-state undergraduate tuition increases and includes $40 million in support for HBCUs Economy/Working Families $79.5 million over the biennium to support increasing the state’s minimum wage to $15/hr. by January 2026 3% salary increase each year of the biennium for state-supported employees $175 million for the Virginia Housing Trust Fund to support the creation or preservation of affordable housing $40 million over the biennium for the Virginia Business Ready Sites Program $243.1 million over the biennium to backfill revenue lost from the removal of the grocery sales tax Health/Mental Health $30.5 million over the biennium to combat the opioid epidemic in our Commonwealth, including... $11 million for the Virginia Department of Health to purchase opioid reversal agents and test kits $400,000 for the Virginia Department of Health to conduct a waster water demonstration project to test for fentanyl and norfentanyl $100,000 to equip public schools with naloxone to combat the deadly opioid epidemic $191.4 million to add additional DD waiver slots and a 3% rate increase each year of the biennium for DD services $126 million over the biennium in additional mental health services $6.2 million over the biennium to fully fund community health workers in local health departments $40 million over the biennium to incentivize higher staffing for nursing homes Public Safety $75.1 million over the biennium for community violence intervention initiatives, including.... $18 million over the biennium for the Community Violence Intervention and Prevention Program $10 million for the Operation Ceasefire Grant Program $7 million over the biennium to add Roanoke to the Safer Communities program $6 million in additional support over the biennium for youth community violence programs $21.3 million over the biennium to backfill and maintain funding for the VOCA Victims Services Grant Program and maintains funding for the Victim Witness, Sexual Assault, and Domestic Violence Victim and Victim Services grant programs Natural Resources/Infrastructure The budget directs the Governor to reenter the Regional Greenhouse Gas Initiative $149.5 million over the biennium to improve our transportation system and ease the burden of the Washington Metropolitan Area Transit Authority (WMATA) $100 million for the Community Flood Preparedness Fund $400 million to meet Chesapeake Bay improvement goals $25 million for the City of Norfolk for Coastal Risk Storm Management Projects Sine Die Every year at the end of session we say the General Sine die directly translates from Latin to “without a day,” and it means without a day planned to come back to session. It designates the end of the official session for part-time legislatures. Although the Latin makes it sound like the phrase originated from Roman times, it actually derives from the 17th century and used to refer to legislative and court proceedings in British and American legal parlance. Despite the phrase being most properly pronounced in Latin as “seen-ay dee-yay,” the most popular pronunciation in the legislature is “sigh-knee dye.” Welcome to Richmond! Thank you so much for stopping by! [] I had the honor of meeting with Lillie Jessie, a former member of the Prince William County School Board. Lillie and her family were in Richmond to accept a commending resolution from the House of Delegates for her tireless service to the students, families, and educators of Prince William County. Thank you, Lillie, for your leadership and congratulations on this well-deserved honor! [] I also had the opportunity to meet with a representative from the Virginia Trucking Association [] to talk about what we can do at the state level to support fleet operators, vendors, and the Commonwealth’s trucking industry. NALOXONE IN EVERY SCHOOL: This bill requires schools to stock and train staff (as well as encourage students to be trained) on the administration of Naloxone, a life-saving medication, in case of an opioid overdose. INVESTIGATE VETERANS AFFAIRS CLAIM SHARKS: This bill convenes a workgroup to investigate predatory Veterans Affairs benefits claim sharks. body:
| [ ](http://links.eaglecoalition.com/u/click?_t=e3c9a4cac7f248a39c2a3d920479a441&_m=3d3192a161634f65b98cc74595d2ea2d&_e=_F8NW-yICJua8kYshhv9iGUOeGd3e_WBNHs_pE0ivqclFkGhQApipYQMEPgxXmtjDlWB1JEPvxHsAmwP0TJFrDA76d_OUipwVYRW-7OaNYiCMLD1hiKXQasceXoZ2l76RLTMq9WTZVwFi_cwXy67BhMXLmoyGiPOLNkZL8aYFVZ2Jv0OFJHJd7PF11BDN-9CntZk-As5zxBiv6IxuIdQR-SplKrTnANGP7Kj8IeS4sWJKjmBmzZb9vYIXcYWXoFT-0JWef5KP9LGditmx9JBtot-5La0LIPt4evxYXw0s09kUFFQbg_4qC9YlHsbwkzSVQ6YsTvItkvtGmdf7UFmANC8mZfaPM36Rv16bMCHMoC3gc2KnOPWY-a3X77jg56cON9HS_tNDgEFx5rqfoChQw%3D%3D)

Friend,

I apologize for asking you so many times. I absolutely can't ignore this anymore.

We're in for the fight of our lives in 2024. We're OFFICIALLY in the election year, and we've got to do everything we can to FIGHT and WIN.

Here's what's happening:

The left will be more nasty and more well-funded than ever before. They will do EVERYTHING and ANYTHING to take out our campaign. I need everyone's support if we're going to win in 2024 and SAVE AMERICA.

subject: Beyond Shocking:
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY TEAM KENNEDY
body:
The political establishment will stop at nothing to prevent Kennedy from being on the ballot.

Dear Peter,
Our campaign's 5th request for Secret Service protection has been officially denied.
Kennedy for President COO Mike Sanders drafted a letter to the Congressional Protection Advisory Committee, where he cited numerous dangerous threats against Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. and his family.
One such incident took place in Los Angeles after our private security team apprehended Adrian Aispuro, who falsely identified himself as a U.S. Marshal and demanded to meet with Kennedy while carrying a loaded handgun, along with a second firearm and ammunition.
Homeland Security guidelines dictate that major presidential candidates who poll with at least 15% support for major candidates or 20% for independent candidates for at least 30 consecutive days should have security.
_Kennedy has been polling _above_ those numbers for months!_
Homeland Security Director Alejandro Mayorkas's response provides no explanation:
_"Based on the facts and the recommendation of the advisory committee, I have determined that Secret Service protection for Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. is not warranted at this time."_
>>>>>Considering the Kennedy family's extensive history of tragedy, including Robert F Kennedy Jr.’s father and uncle, the decision not to provide Secret Service Coverage is beyond shocking.<<<<<
Apparently, the Biden Administration and the political establishment would rather force our campaign to spend millions on security than field organizing, signature collecting, and advertising!
Please support Robert F. Kennedy, Jr.’s campaign today. Not only do we have expensive security costs—$2 million a month!—but we also need to spend more than $20 million to obtain ballot access in all 50 states and the District of Columbia.

subject: should D.C. become the 51st state?
disclaimer_text: Paid for by VPP
body:
we need to hear from you
| | **Republicans Attack DC Sovereignty as Democrats Push for Statehood**
---
The MAGA GOP knows that DC Statehood would almost certainly mean TWO more Democratic Senators, so they’re doing everything in their power to stop it!

If we’re going to have any chance of fighting back, we need to demonstrate MASSIVE grassroots support for DC Statehood! **Please, respond now:**

[**Do you support DC Statehood?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/DCStatehoodPoll_v2/?source=VPP_20240317_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=6&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm)

---

[UNSURE »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/DCStatehoodPoll_v2/?source=VPP_20240317_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=9&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm)
*** PLEASE RESPOND BEFORE 11:59 PM TONIGHT OR YOUR ANSWER WILL AUTOMATICALLY BE MARKED “ NO.” ***
* * *
Listen, not only would DC Statehood give hundreds of thousands of people much-needed representation in Congress, _it could SAVE our slim Senate majority!_

Republicans are TERRIFIED — so they’re launching vicious attacks to shut down any chance we have of making DC Statehood a reality.

**We need 5,000 loyal Democrats to respond by MIDNIGHT:**
[**Do you support DC Statehood?**](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/DCStatehoodPoll_v2/?source=VPP_20240317_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=10&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm)
[YES »](https://act.protectvoting.org/signup/DCStatehoodPoll_v2/?source=VPP_20240317_EMPOLL_E1_T0_AC_RS&t=11&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm)

Thank you,

_VPP_

(www.protectvoting.org)
Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. 401 2nd Ave. S, Ste. 303, Seattle, WA 98104

The Voter Protection Project is on an important mission: To end partisan gerrymandering and voter suppression by electing candidates at the state and federal levels that are forceful advocates for voting rights. Our enfranchisement agenda includes working to ensure that every state has automatic voter registration and online voter registration, convenient early voting, and independent redistricting commissions to ensure politicians are not able to draw their own maps.

Please, consider making a [one-time contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/121?t=1&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm). **Or, to make the most powerful lasting impact and help us oust Republicans up and down the ballot who attack our right to vote, make a[monthly sustaining contribution by clicking here](https://act.protectvoting.org/go/122?t=2&akid=13501%2E878179%2E8i43tm).**

name: =?utf-8?Q?The=20Editors=20at=20Broad=20+=20Liberty?=
subject: B+L Weekly Reads: Remembering "2 Weeks to Flatten the Curve" | School Closures, 4 Years Later + More ⚡🔔
body:
News + editorial to start your week. | | | |
---
| | | Good morning, and welcome to _Broad + Liberty_ 's Weekly Reads.

[Subscribe to our daily email list](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=0d80f8b64b&e=b7b3eddce2) and get the best of _Broad + Liberty_ delivered straight to your inbox.
---
|
### **1.[We will never forget — reflections on the fourth anniversary of school closures](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=3dee66264a&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Beth Ann Rosica_
“On March 13, 2020, changed my life forever.

It was the last normal day when I woke up, sent my kids to school, and started working. By the end of the day, everything had changed. Schools were closed for two weeks to ‘flatten the curve.’ All my kids’ activities were canceled and then restaurants and ‘non-essential’ businesses closed.
I was skeptical immediately, and it took me three weeks to start writing to Governor Wolf about his misguided policies. That is when I became an ‘accidental activist,’ and my life has never been the same.”
**Why It Matters.** “Any parent who lived through virtual schooling witnessed first hand the rapid increase in screen time. School was 100 percent virtual and did not typically provide the requisite number of hours of instruction. Teachers were zoom-fatigued and often ended classes early or allowed students to work when they felt like it. This left more hours in the day for recreational online activities since after school sports and activities were not happening.”

**Quotable.** “We owe it to our children to never allow something like this to happen again. For those of us who stood up against this tyranny, we knew there would be dire consequences. To those who did not speak out and to those who continue to perpetuate this narrative, I implore you to look at what has happened to our children.”

[C](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e473b3efc3&e=b7b3eddce2)[ontinue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=a1cfdc3169&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
### **2.[Lies, damn lies, and two weeks to flatten the curve](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=ced800c993&e=b7b3eddce2)**

_By Guy Ciarrocchi_

“March 13: the fourth anniversary of Pennsylvania’s “two weeks to flatten the curve.”
Governor Tom Wolf issued his first executive order, closing all schools. He would ultimately issue countless executive orders in 2020 and 2021 — including telling us all to stay home and inside without legislative or voter approval.

You may recall that the last of Pennsylvania’s 67 counties was not allowed to go ‘green’ and ‘re-open’ until July 3. That was the ‘thriving metropolis’ of Lebanon County. Why? Because their commissioners had voted not to enforce lockdown punishments in their county. Wolf’s decision underscored his approach: Arrogance. Abuse of Power. Shamelessness.

Ultimately, in the primary of 2021, Pennsylvanians voted for the extraordinary step of amending Pennsylvania’s Constitution to bring his dictatorial madness to an end — and to save our liberties, lives, and livelihoods.”

**Why It Matters**. “Because of our children. Because so much wrong was done to our children — not only in March and April of 2020, but into 2021 and even 2022 — to keep them out of school or masked-up in schools. So much wrong was done to cancel their sports or force them to wear masks while playing softball and basketball, to keep them away from their friends, to keep them away from their grandparents. And it was done with total disregard to their mental and overall physical health. It’s imperative that we remember.

Because those in power stood in judgment and told us that some jobs were ‘essential,’ but others weren’t, forcing millions to stay home and wait for government aid or unemployment checks — sometimes waiting a year. Because even today, there are empty storefronts where small businesses once thrived, it’s imperative that we remember.”

**Quotable**. “Now, the after-the-fact ‘studies.’ Various ‘experts’ and ‘esteemed’ institutions spent millions of dollars and hundreds of hours to produce reports concluding what those of us with commonsense and a sense of liberty and decency knew: the experts blew it. The lockdowns weren’t needed or lasted too long. Millions of people are still suffering the effects of being away from people. Millions of children are way behind academically and emotionally; some may never reach their pre-Covid potential.”

[Continue Reading](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=e92aae143c&e=b7b3eddce2)
---
|
---
|
# **3\. Lightning Round**
* **[Rep. Cartwright takes donations from lobbyist for Saudi Arabia, in contrast to his 2022 attack ads against his Republican opponent](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=15b8c8169a&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Estate of man strangled in Delco prison files federal lawsuit against the county](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=c58b5da4ab&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Andy Bloom: What becomes of the Haley voters?](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=f3c590d0e4&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Rachel Langan: How I became an advocate for school choice](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=f498a60bda&e=b7b3eddce2)**
* **[Michael Thomas Leibrandt: When it comes to the center of the Eagles, Kelce will always be our favorite](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=130cf39bc7&e=b7b3eddce2)**

---
|
---
| _**What We’re Reading...**_
---
| The Supreme Court decision to restore Donald Trump to the ballot in Colorado reveals an unpleasant truth: [the Supreme Court is the last functioning institution in America](https://broadandliberty.us3.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a45553d37b628a10a8ae35af1&id=fe132ff59b&e=b7b3eddce2). So writes David Harsanyi of _The Federalist_ , and convincingly. “No, SCOTUS doesn’t get every case right. Sometimes, led by the chief justice, it is perplexingly Solomonic. But it is wrong within the contours of normal. Congress, on the other hand, has handed its responsibilities on war, spending, and governing to the executive branch. At this point, we are far more likely to see a congressman dunking on someone on social media than acting to defend the document he swore to uphold.”************

clean_body: News + editorial to start your week. | | | | | | | Good morning, and welcome to Broad + Liberty 's Weekly Reads. and get the best of Broad + Liberty delivered straight to your inbox. | 1. By Beth Ann Rosica “On March 13, 2020, changed my life forever. It was the last normal day when I woke up, sent my kids to school, and started working. By the end of the day, everything had changed. Schools were closed for two weeks to ‘flatten the curve.’ All my kids’ activities were canceled and then restaurants and ‘non-essential’ businesses closed. I was skeptical immediately, and it took me three weeks to start writing to Governor Wolf about his misguided policies. That is when I became an ‘accidental activist,’ and my life has never been the same.” Why It Matters. “Any parent who lived through virtual schooling witnessed first hand the rapid increase in screen time. School was 100 percent virtual and did not typically provide the requisite number of hours of instruction. Teachers were zoom-fatigued and often ended classes early or allowed students to work when they felt like it. This left more hours in the day for recreational online activities since after school sports and activities were not happening.” Quotable. “We owe it to our children to never allow something like this to happen again. For those of us who stood up against this tyranny, we knew there would be dire consequences. To those who did not speak out and to those who continue to perpetuate this narrative, I implore you to look at what has happened to our children.” | | 2. By Guy Ciarrocchi “March 13: the fourth anniversary of Pennsylvania’s “two weeks to flatten the curve.” Governor Tom Wolf issued his first executive order, closing all schools. He would ultimately issue countless executive orders in 2020 and 2021 — including telling us all to stay home and inside without legislative or voter approval. You may recall that the last of Pennsylvania’s 67 counties was not allowed to go ‘green’ and ‘re-open’ until July 3. That was the ‘thriving metropolis’ of Lebanon County. Why? Because their commissioners had voted not to enforce lockdown punishments in their county. Wolf’s decision underscored his approach: Arrogance. Abuse of Power. Shamelessness. Ultimately, in the primary of 2021, Pennsylvanians voted for the extraordinary step of amending Pennsylvania’s Constitution to bring his dictatorial madness to an end — and to save our liberties, lives, and livelihoods.” Why It Matters. “Because of our children. Because so much wrong was done to our children — not only in March and April of 2020, but into 2021 and even 2022 — to keep them out of school or masked-up in schools. So much wrong was done to cancel their sports or force them to wear masks while playing softball and basketball, to keep them away from their friends, to keep them away from their grandparents. And it was done with total disregard to their mental and overall physical health. It’s imperative that we remember. Because those in power stood in judgment and told us that some jobs were ‘essential,’ but others weren’t, forcing millions to stay home and wait for government aid or unemployment checks — sometimes waiting a year. Because even today, there are empty storefronts where small businesses once thrived, it’s imperative that we remember.” Quotable. “Now, the after-the-fact ‘studies.’ Various ‘experts’ and ‘esteemed’ institutions spent millions of dollars and hundreds of hours to produce reports concluding what those of us with commonsense and a sense of liberty and decency knew: the experts blew it. The lockdowns weren’t needed or lasted too long. Millions of people are still suffering the effects of being away from people. Millions of children are way behind academically and emotionally; some may never reach their pre-Covid potential.” | | 3\. Lightning Round | | What We’re Reading... | The Supreme Court decision to restore Donald Trump to the ballot in Colorado reveals an unpleasant truth: . So writes David Harsanyi of The Federalist , and convincingly. “No, SCOTUS doesn’t get every case right. Sometimes, led by the chief justice, it is perplexingly Solomonic. But it is wrong within the contours of normal. Congress, on the other hand, has handed its responsibilities on war, spending, and governing to the executive branch. At this point, we are far more likely to see a congressman dunking on someone on social media than acting to defend the document he swore to uphold.”******** | | Broad + Liberty is funded exclusively by readers like you. Your generous support ensures that Broad + Liberty can bring you stories and opinions that Pennsylvania’s mainstream media would rather leave unheard. Please consider making a contribution today. If you would like your gift to be used to help us cover a specific story or subject, please let us know in the contribution form or at mailto:editirs@broadandliberty.com. As always, gifts made to Broad + Liberty are 100% tax deductible! Thank you, dear reader, for your steadfast support of our independent, broad-minded brand of local journalism. We could not do this without you. With gratitude, — The Editors at Broad + Liberty | | "Support Broad + Liberty" | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | | Copyright © 2024 Broad + Liberty, All rights reserved. | | Friend, It's official. We are through game one of the double header. The voters have spoken, and they chose me to be the Republican candidate for Senate in California. That means you and I will have just eight months to build a 58-county, grassroots campaign capable of defeating Adam Schiff - with his _ActBlue_ allies and the war chest he's assembled during the two decades he spent in Washington, D.C. **[That why I'm humbly asking you to rush and emergency $35 contribution to my campaign today.](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=7fd9e65fa27d4c74983ed5f3af9fb605&_e=qereT6nza6cOCpd8xwgWPlwm7oLTwwGNP7I7PkCliR_BCvhGfyDl1v023WFD5WJ7Iptp0h_5v5xUnBstLnhEZPDx7t-LFXmAGycKIxQdQXZtVwouQCofmfVhf826QnrmWteSzc3j4gD0hW8rDmyFVRb2hIt1ULGijV5cUJUhYPnvr5kzNwOyaqh5AA3QMVsq6cxsJD8V9IFSU0sB8hlouyNLXP_oyz1OTBWBLNVHkSNFVJh4TbWliCz9cbAr_BBqn_7pLdeuemPsarTawAYlhOqLqgvEQ1qNLBvasTrL1UqeqqTXqr5Ss7wo4M-DVjh6UhQTXdE9tVtMvVmG00lVtNuzCJI8tkPzbfIVz3bn5nVtnSKn5uEq317G1xtA2P0LXTCo9tdvSFw8afEfs9GsTA%3D%3D)** There's no time to lose. With every day that goes by, you and I are getting more and more reminders why it's so important we win this election. The reality is present-day America is starting to look a lot like 1970s America. You remember what it was like: ► Not only were gas prices sky high, Carter's rationing meant we could only buy gas on even or odd days depending on our license plate numbers. Remember? ► Eleven Israeli athletes were slaughtered at the Olympic Village in Munich by terrorists. Remember? ► Fifty-two Americans were held hostage for 444 days by terrorists in Iran. Remember? Democrat policies had brought America to its knees. And now, as Yogi Berra used to say, **_"It's deja vu all over again."_** Joe Biden, Adam Schiff and the rest of the Democrats don't care one bit that Americans are having a hard time. I'd go even further to say they welcome the misery so they can keep folks down and dependent. You and I _must_ win this race. **[That's why I'm humbly asking you to rush your $35 donation to my campaign today to defeat Adam Schiff in November.](https://links.defend-the-constitution.org/u/click?_t=959d0fa54cad47baa4213176314ae733&_m=7fd9e65fa27d4c74983ed5f3af9fb605&_e=qereT6nza6cOCpd8xwgWPlwm7oLTwwGNP7I7PkCliR_BCvhGfyDl1v023WFD5WJ7Iptp0h_5v5xUnBstLnhEZPDx7t-LFXmAGycKIxQdQXZtVwouQCofmfVhf826QnrmWteSzc3j4gD0hW8rDmyFVRb2hIt1ULGijV5cUJUhYPnvr5kzNwOyaqh5AA3QMVsq6cxsJD8V9IFSU0sB8hlouyNLXP_oyz1OTBWBLNVHkSNFVJh4TbWliCz9cbAr_BBqJEaxyFlWfZsax2rHHDlIYcaMXt8YDU32KGnAJou-8ahAUqhmEQLBMw59GUBthgU6D6kQUz4IH-y7bNCHE5_kzH0jNcdH0VtSJwNi1Zq77NbDssYUevkBj-6DZAojTff363AyV8B0bi1cXVbiNc7O2w%3D%3D)**

body:
Welcome to the latest edition of the DLCC's newsletter, March 17th, 2024 | | |
---
|
---
| [ ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.yjJN_MEbNx94PLaWzZ6hW9WL4ySifRyEQyG7D6ZDcaKmFQcO_SqeUFRkYFn0WrvHHisWiwN-mSj2nwLaaiVAndhLwjC3x9MI2KUAa0DnJViqSOAPtdfRJrzMKtRCwblAbdUzpcIq4TCs0_uSQowl3761YanhkwnwN6GeQTsVOTDpc858BJjNJEjVqskarqp7XcM5gufP1C5RCiqbCX50dkbebcoyfApfwhTYYGiarEJa49Z8tgl4qhCVU8YwbrSVp68h9VbDvnyONoThcmJqCphRhhtt0-D3Jxop4akc7J8eXL-Ggiqb0FckW8fzTQes5mQLKQZWR5dEAjlGEoYH2blgSFiR246kDlR0JueiGWUmKdAj87eo18wq707csWqYt3fPuPQKRro1fuV1_qYHEAmrUvqxo5Gj58mqbrAyJQDbhtmql4Pcb4TznceTdX7b-VvEcUQc2HYpXxHS5pKUlEYYit95-JXxZnm8e32dGKYUGs497zC3aB5jysqUJo9g8u09A0uB3gCC0YyXsuxyEQ/44q/WmC5I-GJQ-OiV8GbcVWHUw/h0/h001.Hjt4OukMg899_Y-mqRD8Q5PAHwpP7KkaWn2sYOJ9mQ0)
---
1️⃣ **_New Hampshire:_ Democrats defend two seats in the state House! **
In New Hampshire, we are celebrating Democratic victories in two special elections in the state House. [These wins were critical to chip away at Republicans’ ultra-thin majority in the New Hampshire House → ](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMYLjHmkCRD59QHrnNPzrYsfCZg1mq22EWpP5ZVsHtRY0MZjXXTk4RUZdOpY7ETmFK0-4E-1gtanzR6yVIaaFVmvPvGnEwnw3SpGjnse6I0u2gHAlDWMLpvUMQ3eGt5fyKqdlRkght3V-muKlhBOFDKJcPoS2BLDk0HPZoLJ7t3vwdrjDWL-hDMpduhWmIT8tOQZWsFMCPWG7V4LrvYhUSW69z7zseNxyP_K4815BTeONvT9q1Q14JR4Rq1omuTkd4lA_QUmi-35rIk7OKkXr8_1DksrvZBRfvFIZuW5W-RRHDdSF5e1DOAcj88lSDdotvjXp4tioNqE5QqJqzXR5VpSnaj-RQ7DwYoq4YHGPpnekV7w7KU-YcWtxIntMp24NywdFwFrDAKT4DVIZLWvceI9rL0eAn5HW8xoj-iAji0wX2P3bEs3Hz558p6C14qEAbI9sch5Vd0mr5hl6NvSmZ7c2QvEpMLz2LHtyjmvD1VabWjSdiNn3U33bYSAcmvxAFf-fWULkngvSTh7pVgYMFMh5mKx_k1oTVIhCqUKvNWiRz6Tkw1TCVmJ0TTe-vpJyEDX3g-Jn7tGZGuMm6D6tibHOzuvhEx0mzapipARX1OSC/44q/WmC5I-GJQ-OiV8GbcVWHUw/h1/h001.pOAbHqxmPaJVIBap0m8oj8wL5N_Ow4UajCq_80Bs3V0)

2️⃣ **_Arizona:_ Republican leader attacks women and contraception in sexist remark **
In Arizona, Senate Majority Leader Sonny Borrelli, who opposes legislation guaranteeing the right to contraceptives, says women can avoid getting pregnant simply by keeping their legs closed. [**This offensive comment showcases the blatant inequality and hate fueling the GOP’s attacks on bodily autonomy and reproductive freedoms →**](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.XaF8mXqsA6b2dSPmhsleMXk_h7fS9_K_5cLQb4qB3YvSX3i03sbQ3W2PQDCT5uU1PfElJKsxefQ3ktof7duoYDEmyfDpOWKZ9np0ZDPsCtGjkn2En_wM3xpidWmhJ9kPI5FvPpkFqipx42aq-0H68p-bEC53q-Yg4wO4i4Pb-KrxtEitha86g6W13oPYWUrAOlXsP2l75cA_lE4P14xAYhgdWwwReiH_mbwrK4E8NcVENFYTnCcyehrJBOcG2wKQNTIW77Q-YP3NCb0qiE8CcygyoNt8A3domKdgJnAoO8bSRa-GtrHZGXVLMZ8hvp7lVXVj-wbtmLJ0iZr0CJT4qm2QSKee86D1G4Yb_JB0GZGVI9zGM5EFCeJq_JTyPk2H54BqUb17uB4Gx1ThaqjhLH2RuqGLUsMw9PKYNSL79bZc4EG2x6A_1MiWUXziM6qd4z6chJosIRteJ0A1t_zH_1GGteGUN2-O2LBaHPP1WvxbyfiSGJrIZW-Etx7xcGLYbAA0LxERpFS1Oo4Gk9tz9s6GhYS_EBjuNlQvMDe3xvX8ihQj0AhWkdMHBa9rPDBdNZdWwetZol-CW435AqrH3A/44q/WmC5I-GJQ-OiV8GbcVWHUw/h2/h001.XQUEBG1sGSskn4B9mUaMqA35B7Xh-sKjTiGkR-2ZIbc)
3️⃣ **_Iowa:_ Republican lawmakers advanced a bill that could have criminalized IVF methods **
In Iowa, following last month’s controversial Alabama Supreme Court ruling affecting IVF, Republicans advanced legislation that would threaten reproductive care and IVF by criminalizing causing the death of an “unborn” fetus. [**Though tabled after Democratic outrage, this devastating bill suggests life sentences for doctors who have ‘caused the death of an unborn fetus’ →**](https://an.dlcc.org/ss/c/u001.qUXRBnqZ7T8nxbpAcIVwoc5lroAYgnBM-pxs59wzI1SxA9fgw56ryv_mu_sZRYY7jHSIylQN6QsZg0I5045eXNdex60IbzanNjr-bPvLQQ8zo-FGguwah2AaJBePm8H7BTffumgeU9leD9Ue04FDd6VF538nGcaJ3XlybKhAUhtr-_Oj4bDY7FD7NWqdxRUz8ub3jK1bEzzOuVA5axyLbF5vWueY03DA5H_HXRIcfCzWPcbURfYxTNuOiwkCXt5brzl84zE5h6XlZhTtekxOUs_uQvffi2GsfybA1n3E7nR-jhD5MZmXo-q5TeXB8C7QUcsDpvmbWgNsD-6zFYftwCVXJbB-YblMYu_Fworqp8FMlPdkwlbYyNCLC5K0fjKjx0Gy3JXTsYqY6LqtwoY8IdceM3JuZFDqeI-_wZIK8kt_lOXWo0o1l6gpRI_F2UJCxTUntERHc8CTbx28x9GWWajL9uov2rX_eWoD2fSJ9_oUhktkryDJsUGcRUZQnRTgXgR0cacJRO3SWAjUb1w1Vo19CYXAKLsR9qeVCexR0GIMLdylBUzjBmO-oqpHtXOotMPSKa4Set1j6pKxVd7RBXA66r6cLUMj8eOJ3gou9-x4nS6vYky9Ek51hs1ixj9f/44q/WmC5I-GJQ-OiV8GbcVWHUw/h3/h001.gwCt-u2l630H-8kLYGZihjKWxxotKX9PWcQkxm_Z_VQ)

State Democrats are facing critical fights ahead. All the way back in 1870, Hiram Rhodes Revels, a pastor and State Senator from Mississippi, was elected as the first Black U.S. Senator. Since then, we've come a long way as a country. **But representation – like our democracy – is a work in progress, and I believe we can do better.**

Black Americans have long faced far _too many_ obstacles like, discrimination, lower pay, and higher job instability – not to mention the racist voter suppression laws that Texas Republicans like Ted Cruz have championed, which make it harder for certain folks to vote.
**The bottom line is this: It's essential that our government reflects the people it represents.**
I'm ready to defeat Ted Cruz this November and fight to build a more perfect union for my boys and every kid in Texas. But unlike Ted Cruz, I don't have corporate special interests footing the bill for my campaign.

**To win, I need a massive movement of grassroots supporters to step up and join me in this fight.[Please, will you chip in $5 to split between my campaign and Jacky Rosen's to help me defeat Ted Cruz, become the first Black Senator from Texas, and be the leader Texans deserve? →](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/81116280/461446003/1682731468?amounts=250,100,50,25,5&amount=5&refcode=jtk3587-bu-nd&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9OR1AvTkdQNDAvMS84ODI5OCIsDQogICJEaXN0cmlidXRpb25VbmlxdWVJZCI6ICI4YTAzMjhhYi01ZmU0LWVlMTEtYWFmMC0wMDIyNDgyMjM3OTQiLA0KICAiRW1haWxBZGRyZXNzIjogImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIg0KfQ%3D%3D&hmac=c2eNuyStlPgSzC0RmNcfcvsiWu9tff9CFOCgWr5mzAU=)**

clean_body: It's essential that our government reflects the people it represents. | | | = Friend, I'm running to defeat Ted Cruz and become the first-ever Black Senator from Texas. = | | = All the way back in 1870, Hiram Rhodes Revels, a pastor and State Senator from Mississippi, was elected as the first Black U.S. Senator. Since then, we've come a long way as a country. But representation – like our democracy – is a work in progress, and I believe we can do better. Black Americans have long faced far too many obstacles like, discrimination, lower pay, and higher job instability – not to mention the racist voter suppression laws that Texas Republicans like Ted Cruz have championed, which make it harder for certain folks to vote. The bottom line is this: It's essential that our government reflects the people it represents. I'm ready to defeat Ted Cruz this November and fight to build a more perfect union for my boys and every kid in Texas. But unlike Ted Cruz, I don't have corporate special interests footing the bill for my campaign. To win, I need a massive movement of grassroots supporters to step up and join me in this fight.= If you've saved payment information with ActBlue Express, your donation split between Colin Allred and Jacky Rosen will go through immediately: | | | = | | = | | = | | = I'm so grateful to have you fighting with me. – Colin | *

body:
Please share this with as many people as you can. Share with 5-10 people. | | | [](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Fwww.alaskansforhonestelections.com%2F%3Fss_source%3Dsscampaigns%26ss_campaign_id%3D65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471%26ss_email_id%3D65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0%26ss_campaign_name%3DWe%25E2%2580%2599ve%2Bfought%2BRCV%2Bfor%2Ba%2Byear%252C%2BDon%25E2%2580%2599t%2BStop%26ss_campaign_sent_date%3D2024-03-17T17%253A27%253A25Z&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=i_II0F6sOH7Al_Rm1FtTGUTr1xw%3D)

|

**Celebrating Our Repeal of**[**#RankedChoiceVoting**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fhashtag%2FRankedChoiceVoting%3Fsrc%3Dhashtag_click&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=y7HsAoFeYKzDL6cMhftRsiu0NTw%3D)**in Fairbanks, these two ladies led Fairbanks. They made sure we had thousands of signatures more than we needed. In many of their districts we were over 480% of the required signatures, most in the State. In some districts they turned in nearly 3,000 signatures for a goal of 500 in a district. They were relentless and they supported me since Day 1.**
---
---
|
---
---
|
---
**We will be at the District 31-36 Republican Convention in Fairbanks Today. We are going to Update 150 Fairbanks Republicans on Our Fight Against**[**#rankedchoicevoting**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2Fhashtag%2Frankedchoicevoting%3Fsrc%3Dhashtag_click&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=VSGquiWUooEq_IiXRVRXmnnvkNA%3D)**If you are in District 31-36 make sure to attend 1201 Hoselton Rd, Fairbanks, AK Pay for your ticket here**[**https://alaskagop.net/events/**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Ft.co%2Fc4eur0teIL&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=9bpiVYa4ta0wc0N9r3IpavJVVo4%3D)**District 1, 8, 25, 26, 27, 28, 31, 32, 33, 34, 35, 36 Convention is Tomorrow March 16th. If you need to know what your district is, go to this link**[**https://akleg.gov/**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Fakleg.gov%2F&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=UHrrfHpUzJ7_SVX0_3nbfF5SBaI%3D)**and scroll to the bottom and type in your address and it will tell you who your State Senator and State House of Representative is, whatever district your State House of Representatives is, is your district number. Search on**[**https://alaskagop.net/events/**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Ft.co%2Fc4eur0teIL&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=9bpiVYa4ta0wc0N9r3IpavJVVo4%3D)**for your district convention.**
---
---
|
---
#### Sign Up To Join [Scott Presler ](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Ftwitter.com%2FScottPresler&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=TeEH9TaYDxVwiVpCTtLWRC5lDL0%3D) this Fall, We plan to take him Silver Salmon Fishing, We plan to Take Him on a Glacier Tour, and We plan on going to the Alaska State Fair. If you would like to stay updated on Our Plans, Want to Meet Scott Presler, or if you would like us to come to your group or community event, please reach out and we will see what we can schedule. Planning Mid to Late August for him to be in Town. Dates Coming Soon, Stay Tuned.
---
---
|
---
### **We ran a poll for 7 days, here are the results**
---
---
|
---
#### **Who’s Team are You on?**
#### **Team Alaska or Team Soros**
#### WE DID IT!!!, We are in [Ballotpedia!](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Fballotpedia.org%2FAlaska_Repeal_Top-Four_Ranked-Choice_Voting_Initiative_%25282024%2529&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=j_BoYNeGfrHu0eowV6y2k4-Vwco%3D)
#### **Scott Presler might be coming to Alaska to help us GOTV for our Repeal of Rank Choice Voting**
---
---
|
---
---
|
---
#### **There are some that feel I am being too mean to the GOP Leadership. I do not think so, I think they should be accountable for their inaction (No Assistance on the Repeal of Ranked Choice Voting). The Arizona GOP, Idaho GOP, Hawaii GOP all invited me with Open Arms, The Alaska GOP was nonexistent.**
#### **I am not talking about local GOP groups, or individuals. I am discussing the Alaska GOP Leadership. Why the obvious disdain for Citizen Driven Politics?**
#### **We received more help from other State’s GOP. The Alaska GOP Leadership did not help us, let’s get a real leader. Jerry Ward**
#### **Out of all the candidates running to be GOP Chairman, Jerry Ward was essential to our success, we support Jerry Ward for Chairman of the Alaska GOP.**
#### **Learn how to be a delegate and vote in your district for Jerry Ward for GOP Chairman.**
---
### [**GOP Calendar**](https://f69e.engage.squarespace-mail.com/r?m=65f727eeb0dba30f303f39a0&u=https%3A%2F%2Falaskagop.net%2Fevents%2Fmonth%2F2024-03%2F&w=636d98a80d1d302f275cb244&c=b_65f726a2ac4b56030a0c7471&l=en-US&s=Afw8u_KsTPuOUX5x3sGtK4Trgp4%3D)

|
